text:
Market today: The mood is cautious with inflation broadening out and Treasury yields rising due to Middle East tensions. This creates headwinds for growth-oriented tech and chip names, as higher rates can impact valuations and borrowing costs for margin.

Your holdings: SOXL (-46.77%) and SPAL (-47.83%) are in critical territory; their stops (SOXL: 200, SPAL: 21.38) are significantly above current prices, indicating substantial losses already. QQQ (-2.7%), SMH (-12.55%), and QQQI (-3.9%) are also near their stops. Remember, margin amplifies these drawdowns.

Watch: Keep a close eye on chip sector sentiment, as Wall Street is divided on whether a buying opportunity is nearing. Also, monitor the broader tech market given the "Magnificent Seven" narrative is fading.

Bottom line: Focus on risk management for your highly concentrated, leveraged positions to prevent further capital erosion.
plays: Address SOXL and SPAL immediately. Their stops are well past, indicating significant losses; consider exiting or re-evaluating your risk exposure.
Respect stops on QQQ (670), SMH (540), and QQQI (52) to manage downside on core positions.
No new buys today - hold and watch. While chip stocks are on the radar, the market sentiment is divided, and existing positions need attention first.
